lines.en_lit: Just as below by means of dissection and not by means of skinning, so too here by means of dissection and not by means of skinning.

And there, from where do we As it was taught and its innards, and its dung, shall he carry teaches that he brings it out whole. might that he should burn it whole. It is stated here: Its head and its legs, and it is stated there, head and legs. Just as there, by means of dissection, so too here it is by means of dissection.

If Just as there, with skinning, so too here with skinning, the verse states “and its innards, and its dung.” What is the derivation Rav Pappa said: Just as its dung is within its innards so too, its flesh is in its skin
at what point the render the garments impure. The Sages taught shall be taken outside the camp and they shall burn”
Below, you give them three camps, And here you give them one camp, If so, why is it stated: “Outside the camp”? to say to you: Once it has left one camp renders the garments impure.
And there, from where do we As it was taught “Even the whole bull shall he carry outside outside of three camps.

you say outside of three camps, or is outside of only one camp? When it says the bull the community: outside the camp” as no the verse to state since it was already stated: “And burn it as he burned the first bull” And what the verse states outside the camp? To give it a second camp,
And when it says: “Outside the camp” ash, as no the verse to state since it was already stated where the ashes are poured out give it a third camp,

And what does Rabbi Shimon do with outside the camp, He needs it for that which was taught Rabbi Eliezer says: It is stated here, outside the camp, and it is stated there, “outside the camp” Just as here, outside three camps, so too there outside three camps. And just as there east of Jerusalem, so too here east of Jerusalem.
And according to the Rabbis, where do they burn them? As it was taught Where are they burned?
